input.command:
# Generate new key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/new-tls.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key /tmp/new-tls.key -out /tmp/new-tls.csr -config <(cat <<'EOF'
[ req ]
default_bits       = 2048
prompt             = no
default_md         = sha256
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions     = req_ext

[ dn ]
CN = demo.example.com

[ req_ext ]
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[ alt_names ]
DNS.1 = demo.example.com
EOF
) 2>/dev/null

# Sign the CSR with the CA using a tempdir (matching the precondition pattern)
D=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$D/newcerts"
: > "$D/index.txt"
echo 1000 > "$D/serial"
cat > "$D/ca.cnf" <<CONF
[ca]
default_ca=CA_default
[CA_default]
dir=$D
database=$D/index.txt
new_certs_dir=$D/newcerts
serial=$D/serial
certificate=/tmp/test-ca.crt
private_key=/tmp/test-ca.key
default_md=sha256
policy=pol
[pol]
commonName=supplied
CONF

openssl ca -batch -config "$D/ca.cnf" -days 730 \
  -extfile <(cat <<'EOF'
subjectAltName = DNS:demo.example.com
EOF
) \
  -in /tmp/new-tls.csr -out /tmp/new-tls.crt 2>&1
rm -rf "$D"

# Verify the new cert
openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -ext subjectAltName -in /tmp/new-tls.crt 2>/dev/null
